title: Smoky Pillowy Middle-Eastern Buns Maqluba
description: A festive Middle-Eastern breakfast dish featuring soft and fluffy buns with a smoky flavor, often served in a maqluba form. Maqluba is a traditional Arab dish that means 'upside down'.

ingredients.name: All-purpose Flour
ingredients.notes: warm water (around 100-110°F) to proof the dough, and some extra for kneading.

ingredients.name: Smoked Paprika
ingredients.notes: to add a smoky flavor to the buns.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine yeast, flour, salt, sugar, and warm water.
Mix the dough until it comes together, then knead for 10 minutes and let it rise for 1 hour.
In a separate bowl, mix together olive oil, smoked paprika, rosemary leaves, garlic powder, and sesame seeds.
Preheat a maqluba pan or a 9x13-inch baking dish to 375°F (190°C).
Shape the risen dough into small balls and flatten them slightly into buns.
Place the buns in the preheated pan, brush them with the smoky paprika mixture, and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Bake the buns in the preheated pan for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the buns from the pan and let them cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes before serving.
